questions.text: What is the primary reason DeFi protocols can interact with each other so seamlessly (composability)?

options.text: They all use the same programming language (Solidity).
They share common token standards (like ERC-20) and exist on the same blockchain, allowing atomic transactions between them.
They are all owned by the same company.
They charge the same fees.

questions.explanation: Composability comes from standardization and shared settlement. All protocols use the same asset types and can call each other within a single transaction atomically.
questions.modelAnswer:
✅ Correct: B

Composability comes from standardization and shared settlement. All protocols use the same asset types and can call each other within a single transaction atomically.

questions.text: Which layer of the DeFi stack is responsible for executing smart contracts and providing security?

options.text: The Settlement Layer (the blockchain)
The Asset Layer
The Aggregation Layer
The Application Layer

questions.explanation: The blockchain (Ethereum, MST) is the foundation that executes all smart contracts and guarantees finality. It's the trust layer of the stack.
questions.modelAnswer:
✅ Correct: A

The blockchain (Ethereum, MST) is the foundation that executes all smart contracts and guarantees finality. It's the trust layer of the stack.

questions.text: What does TVL (Total Value Locked) represent in DeFi?

options.text: The total number of users of a protocol.
The total dollar value of all assets deposited into a protocol's smart contracts.
The total fees collected by a protocol.
The total number of transactions processed.

questions.explanation: TVL measures the capital locked in a protocol. It's a key metric for trust and liquidity depth.
questions.modelAnswer:
✅ Correct: B

TVL measures the capital locked in a protocol. It's a key metric for trust and liquidity depth.

questions.text: Why is MST considered a superior settlement layer for DeFi compared to Ethereum?

options.text: MST offers drastically lower gas fees (~₹0.006 per transaction) and higher throughput, making micro-transactions and small-scale DeFi participation economically viable.
MST does not support smart contracts, making it simpler.
MST has a larger TVL than Ethereum.
MST is a Layer-2 solution for Ethereum.

questions.explanation: MST's key advantage is cost and speed, without sacrificing EVM compatibility. This democratizes access to DeFi.
questions.modelAnswer:
✅ Correct: A

MST's key advantage is cost and speed, without sacrificing EVM compatibility. This democratizes access to DeFi.

options.text: A loan that is borrowed and repaid within the same transaction, requiring no collateral, made possible by atomic composability.
A loan that requires double the collateral.
A loan that can only be used for trading.
A loan that is instantly approved by a bank.

questions.explanation: Flash loans are unique to DeFi and rely on the ability to bundle multiple protocol interactions into one atomic transaction. If the loan isn't repaid by the end, the entire transaction reverts.
questions.modelAnswer:
✅ Correct: A

Flash loans are unique to DeFi and rely on the ability to bundle multiple protocol interactions into one atomic transaction. If the loan isn't repaid by the end, the entire transaction reverts.
